channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Transfer: Konyaspor hold talks with Awaziem over summer move
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Turkish Super Lig club Konyaspor have held talks with Nigerian defender Chidozie Awaziem, DAILY POST reports. Awaziem is expected to leave French club Nantes this summer following the Canaries relegation last season. According to Turkish news outlet, Spor Depor, Konyaspor are keen to secure the serv

item.title: SERAP to Akpabio, Abbas: Withdraw social media shutdown bill or face legal action
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: SERAP warned that the proposal would give the Federal Government sweeping powers to exclude platforms from The post SERAP to Akpabio, Abbas: Withdraw social media shutdown bill or face legal action appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: You helped sharpen democracy — Omokri’s birthday tribute to Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Nigeria’s Ambassador-designate to Mexico, Reno Omokri, has congratulated the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, on his 65th birthday.

item.title: Police, corps members aid election rigging, says Deji Adeleke
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …Warns Electoral Malpractices, Weak Rule of Law Scare Investors Away By Shina Abubakar, Osogbo Billionaire businessman and founder of Adeleke University, Dr Deji Adeleke, has expressed concern over electoral malpractices and weak adherence to the rule of law, warning that such challenges are discour

item.title: Arsenal Linked With Shock Move For Former Man City Star
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Arsenal are considering John Stones as a short-term freebie fix if William Saliba’s injury proves to be serious. Saliba’s suspected back issue is yet to be assessed by the Gunners but there are fears he could be out for up to five months. The 25-year-old was forced off with the long-standing problem

item.title: Our Resolve To Send Joel-Onowakpo To Senate Paying Off – IDU President
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President of the Isoko Development Union (IDU) Worldwide, Professor Chris Akpotu, has said the decision of the Isoko people to elect Senator Joel-Onowakpo Thomas to represent Delta South Senatorial District is already yielding significant dividends, citing President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s assent

item.title: Full List: Countries that have won back-to-back World Cups
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Winning the FIFA World Cup is the ultimate achievement in football. Defending that title, however, has proven even more difficult. The post Full List: Countries that have won back-to-back World Cups appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: There was palpable excitement as the winners of 2026 edition of the annual national essay competition held in honour of Nobel Laureate, Professor Wole Soyinka, were unveiled on Wednesday at an award ceremony. Held at Ijegba Forest, Kemta Estate, Idi-Aba, Abeokuta, Ogun State, the event was part of t

item.description: …Commends EFCC’s recent court victories By Chioma Obinna The Human Rights Writers Association of Nigeria (HURIWA) on Sunday called for greater transparency and public oversight in the management of assets recovered through anti-corruption efforts, saying Nigerians deserve to know how forfeited prope

item.title: Group calls for due process ahead of Abia polls
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A political pressure group, the Abia Solidarity Forum, has called on anti-corruption and security agencies to remain impartial and uphold due process as political activities gather momentum in Abia State. The post Group calls for due process ahead of Abia polls appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Terrorist Attack Kills 16 Soldiers In Western Niger Republic
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Sixteen soldiers have been killed and 23 others wounded in a terrorist attack targeting a military deployment in the Tera area of Tillaberi region in western Niger Republic, the country’s ANP state news agency reported on Sunday. “A terrorist attack targeting a military deployment in the

item.title: Ooni of Ife, Oba Ogunwusi loses elder brother, Prince Adetunji
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Ogunwusi, Ojaja II, has lost his immediate elder brother, Prince Adetunji Ogunwusi. The monarch made the announcement in a statement through the Director of Media and Public Affairs, Ooni’s Palace, Sodiq Lawal, on Sunday, describing the elder brother as a distinguished e

item.title: Atiku, Obi Lack Grassroots Structures, Northern Appeal To Unseat Tinubu – Ganduje

item.description: The immediate past National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Dr. Umar Abdullahi Ganduje, has boasted that the presidential candidates of the NDC and ADC, Peter Obi and Alhaji Atiku Abubakar, do not have the grassroots structure and regional appeal required to “penetrate the Nor

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Former Anambra State Governor and presidential hopeful, Mr. Peter Obi, has marked his 65th birthday with a message of reflection, gratitude to God, and a renewed commitment to service, urging Nigerians to place greater emphasis on improving the lives of others rather than celebrating personal milest

item.title: Mary Habila: Senator Katung mourns, calls for thorough investigation
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senator Sunday Marshall Katung, representing Kaduna South Senatorial District, has expressed deep sorrow over the death of Nurse Mary Habila, describing the incident as a tragic loss that has thrown her family and the wider community into mourning. The post Mary Habila: Senator Katung mourns, calls

item.title: Century-old TB vaccine may reduce insulin needs in Type 1 diabetes — Researchers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A vaccine originally developed to prevent tuberculosis more than a century ago may help people with Type 1 diabetes use The post Century-old TB vaccine may reduce insulin needs in Type 1 diabetes — Researchers appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: The FBI reportedly won’t investigate ICE anymore
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: According to the New York Times, federal agents have been told that the FBI will no longer be investigating confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ denied the change in policy to The Times. The reported change in guidance follows renewed scrutiny of violence by ICE agents who have kille

item.title: Minimum Wage: We’re battle ready for major national struggle — NLC vows
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigeria Labour Congress, NLC, has vowed that it would continue its national struggle for a thorough review of the national minimum wage. The NLC President, Joe Ajaero, said this at an event by pensioners in Abuja. According to him, it is no longer acceptable to discuss the welfare of workers wit

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Sahara Group Foundation has commissioned two new Sahara Go Recycling Hubs in Jigawa and Kaduna States, extending structured waste management and economic opportunity to communities in Northern Nigeria. The hubs, at Gidan Hakimi, Shuwarin Local Government Area, Jigawa State, and Asharami Retail Stati

item.description: I like to think that I’m a citizen of the world. item.description: By Cynthia Alo For more than five decades, crude oil has remained the dominant pillar of Nigeria’s economy, displacing agriculture from the position it once occupied as the country’s major source of revenue and foreign exchange. item.description: Rory McIlroy has set his sights on a strong finish to the PGA Tour and DP World Tour seasons after being ‘stuck in neutral’ during a disappointing week at The 154th Open.

item.description: The presidential candidate of Nigeria Democratic Congress (ADC) Peter Obi, marked his 65th birthday on Sunday with a N10m donation to a rural hospital in his home state of Anambra, saying birthdays should be “moments of deep reflection” rather than occasions for elaborate celebration. item.description: Iran’s military has said it shot down an American LUCAS drone in the southern part of the country. item.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer has predicted that Argentina will defeat Spain in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final.

item.title: Why I said Lagos doesn’t need toy gov like Fashola – Obanikoro
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Minister of State for Defence, Musiliu Obanikoro, has explained why he once said that Lagos State does not need a toy governor like Babatunde Fashola. While contesting against Fashola in the Lagos State governorship election in 2007, Obanikoro had said: “Lagos does not need a toy govern

item.title: FIFA Makes U-Turn, Changes Length Of World Cup 2026 Final Half-Time Show
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: FIFA has limited the World Cup 2026 final halftime break to 17 minutes, including an 11-minute musical performance. This decision serves as a major relief for Spain and Argentina, as initial projections feared the Super Bowl-style stage setup could stretch the intermission to as long as 30 minutes.

item.title: Analysed: Was Hamilton's five-second penalty for Russell collision fair?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Karun Chandhok was at the SkyPad to analyse whether Lewis Hamilton should have been awarded a five-second penalty for his collision with George Russell on the opening lap.

item.title: Israel uses law to destroy Palestinian education in East Jerusalem
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Legislation recently passed by the Knesset is meant to dismantle the Palestinian education system in East Jerusalem.

item.title: Tropical Storm Watches Posted Across Florida’s Panhandle Region
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A tropical storm watch has been issued for the Florida Panhandle as the Atlantic’s next event takes shape off shore.

item.title: Is there a deadline to claim the $1,000 ‘Trump account’ contribution for my kid?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Some children who don’t qualify for the government contribution may be eligible for an initial $250.

item.title: World Cup Final: La Roja, La Albiceleste battle for glory
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: La Roja's patient possession game meets the Albiceleste's trademark resilience in the World Cup final in New York on Sunday. As Spain coach Luis de la Fuente and his former coaching student Lionel Scaloni, now Argentina's manager, prepare to face off on football's biggest stage, the showdown promise

item.title: US and Iran trade fire after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

item.title: Antonelli takes big Belgian GP win as Russell's woes deepen after Hamilton clash
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Kimi Antonelli overcame an unfortunately-timed Virtual Safety Car to overtake Charles Leclerc and win the Belgian Grand Prix on a day team-mate George Russell's title hopes suffered a major fresh blow.

item.title: Belgian Grand Prix | Race Highlights
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Highlights from the 2026 Belgian Grand Prix.

item.title: My wife and I never imagined we’d become Ogun’s first family — Gov Abiodun
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Ogun State Governor, Prince Dapo Abiodun, has said neither he nor his wife ever imagined they would one day become the First Family of Ogun State when The post My wife and I never imagined we’d become Ogun’s first family — Gov Abiodun appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Article of Faith: The battle is the Lord’s, By Femi Aribisala
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: I decided to confront the Director-General with the news that my God would remove him from office through a television announcement. In 1986, I was appointed Special Adviser to Nigeria’s Minister of External Affairs, Professor Bolaji Akinyemi. When I was offered the job, my boss at the Nigerian Inst

item.title: World Cup 2026: He Did Everything Right –Tuchel Hails Saka’s Hattrick Against France
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel has hailed Bukayo Saka after scoring a hat-trick in a thrilling 6-4 victory over France in the bronze medal match of the FIFA World Cup 2026 in Miami. In the post-match, Tuchel made a humorous admission that he was so caught up in the flow and demands of the match that

item.title: Highlights: England smash 387-3 vs India in ODI series decider
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Highlights of the first innings from the third ODI match between England and India.

item.title: Peter Obi Names Education, Health As Most Critical Sectors of Any Nation’s Economy
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: * Donates N10m to mission hospital to mark birthday  David-Chyddy Eleke in Awka  Former Anambra State Governor and presidential candidate of Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Mr Peter Obi, has said

item.title: President Tinubu congratulates Segun Olulade at 55
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: President Tinubu also commended Olulade’s contributions to national development, youth empowerment, enterprise development, and commitment to a cleaner Lagos.
